subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Title succinctly conveys topic and scope, setting clear context.
Table of contents outlines sections effectively and aids navigation.
Clear introduction, body, and conclusion with logical progression of models.
weaknesses: Section numbering errors (duplicate â2.â) and mis-labelled figures hinder clarity.
Occasional digressions (long WHO quotations) add unnecessary bulk.
Combined figure captions and skipped figure numbers disrupt flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three presentation strands were assessed at band 3: the exploration is coherent and logically developed, well organized with clear structure, and mostly concise with relevant content. Minor numbering and labelling lapses prevent a band 4, so a score of 3/4 accurately reflects the quality.

strengths: Uses standard notation (e.g. y = ab^x) and defines multiple graphical representations (tables, scatterplots, overlay graphs).
Effective use of multiple forms of representation (regression curves, bar charts of percentage error).
Logical step-by-step presentation of modelling choices and results.
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (plain-text superscripts, misuse of degree symbol) and undefined variables.
Missing axis labels and units on some figures.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication shows some appropriate language and notation (band 2) but overall effective multiple representations (band 3) and good logical structure (band 3). The combination of strengths and weaknesses aligns with a band 3 rating, meriting 3/4.

strengths: Choice of a personally relevant public-health topic shows significant independent thought.
Adaptation of a UK model to Mexico demonstrates clear personal approach.
Inclusion of percentage-error comparisons and currency conversions exhibits creativity and initiative.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on calculator outputs limits demonstration of original derivation.
Approach is straightforward and follows standard modelling techniques.
No particularly novel mathematical insight or innovative method.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three personal engagement strands meet the âgoodâ descriptor (band 2): evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach and creativity, but not outstanding or highly innovative. A score of 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Reflects on model fit with RÂ² values and percentage error throughout.
Thoughtful analysis of results and process, including linking findings to tax policy.
Evaluates strengths and limitations and suggests multivariable regression as improvement.
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive without deep probing of assumptions or biases.
Lacks critical evaluation of data-source reliability and justification of error thresholds.
Future implications and methodological critiques are mentioned only briefly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present and meaningful (band 2) with thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation, but it lacks the depth and insight required for band 3. A score of 2/3 accurately captures this level.

strengths: Relevant exponential and quadratic models used purposefully to address the research question.
Consistent application of percentage-error analysis and currency conversion in model selection.
Clear quantitative reasoning in choosing and adapting models to the data.
weaknesses: Early regression produced an invalid negative base; fitting procedure needs review.
Minor errors in percentage-error formula and lack of rationale for rounding exponents.
Unit differences (pack sizes) not adjusted in comparative tables.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the mathematics employed is appropriate and demonstrates a sound SL-level understanding (band 3), its purposeful and effective application across modelling, error analysis and real-world conversions justifies a band 4 rating in use of mathematics. A score of 4/6 appropriately reflects this effective use.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are presented step-by-step (data â regression choice â error test â model selection), making the mathematical reasoning followable.  Minor algebraic slips and occasional awkward wording affect absolute clarity, but overall the logical structure is good and ideas are communicated clearly.

Calculations are largely correct; graphs match equations and conversions are accurate.  Minor errors include an incorrect percentage-error formula in text and an impossible negative base for an exponential found in early hand work, but these do not materially affect final results.  Overall understanding is good with minor slips, placing the work in band 3.

The student employs tables, scatterplots, several regression curves, bar charts of percentage error and comparative overlay graphs for the two countriesâdemonstrating effective use of multiple representations.  Each representation is generally appropriate and aids interpretation; however scales are not always stated and some images are blurry, so the work meets but does not exceed the âeffectiveâ descriptor.

The exploration follows a clear narrative (introduction â modelling â adaptation â evaluation â conclusion).  Paragraphs and headings generally flow in a sensible order and the reader can follow the development of each model without back-tracking.  Some minor numbering errors (duplicate â2.â sections) and occasional digressions (e.g. long quotations on WHO policy) prevent the piece from reaching the highest level, but overall logical progression and coherence are sound.

Introducing percentage-error comparisons, currency conversion, and scale-factor manipulations shows creativity and initiative, but these ideas are standard techniques in modelling.  There is no novel mathematical technique or particularly original insight, so the work fits the âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ band.

The analysis of results is thoughtfulâcomparing regression types, interpreting error measures, and linking price increases to tax policyâbut stops short of rigorous statistical justification or sensitivity testing.  This level aligns with âthoughtful analysisâ (band 2).

The student evaluates model strengths (predictive ability, high RÂ²) and limitations (single-variable assumption, data comparability) and suggests multivariable regression as an improvement.  The evaluation is clear but not comprehensive; future implications are mentioned briefly, placing the work in band 2.

The student reflects on model fit (RÂ² values, percentage error) and acknowledges data-source reliability issues and external factors such as healthcare advances.  Reflection is present throughout but remains descriptive and does not probe assumptions or limitations deeply, corresponding to âmeaningfulâ rather than âsubstantialâ reflection.

Standard notation (e.g. y = ab^x) and symbols are used, but there are noticeable flaws: the exponential constant is sometimes written with a minus sign that makes no sense, powers are shown as superscripts in plain text ("ax2"), and Â° appears instead of exponent for one equation.  Variables are not always defined consistently (switch between y and u).  These issues reduce consistency, placing the work in the âsome appropriateâ band.

The adaptation step (scaling the exponential model for Mexico) and discussion of economic context illustrate a personal approach that goes beyond a textbook exercise, yet the treatment is straightforward and not deeply innovative.  Hence a solid but not exceptional level of personal approach is demonstrated.

Most paragraphs are relevant to constructing, justifying or evaluating the models, and the total length (â13 pages) is appropriate.  Some repetition occurs (multiple explanations of the exponential form; WHO quotations repeated) and the hand-calculation section that is later discarded adds unnecessary bulk, so the exploration is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.

All mathematics employed (exponential & quadratic functions, simultaneous equations, percentage error, currency conversion, scaling factors) is relevant to the task.  While limited in breadth, it is appropriate and used purposefully, meriting the descriptor ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3).

Choosing a personally relevant public-health topic and deciding to adapt a UK model to Mexico shows significant independent thought; however, modelling relies heavily on calculator outputs with limited original derivation.  Thus evidence of independent thinking is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

A recognisable introduction, body and conclusion are present, supported by sub-headings, tables and numbered figures.  The table of contents matches most sections and graphs are embedded close to the text that discusses them.  Minor lapses (mis-labelled figures, inconsistent numbering) show that organisation is good rather than exemplary.

The techniques are fully commensurate with SL levelâstandard function modelling, basic statistics and unit conversions.  No HL or advanced material is attempted, but the mathematics clearly meets SL expectations, so band 3 (âcommensurate with course levelâ) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The title succinctly conveys the topic and scope of the investigation, providing clear context and focus for the reader.
The table of contents effectively outlines the major sections and subsections, aiding navigation and reinforcing organizational clarity.
Figures 1 and 2 are wellâplaced next to the raw data table, with clear captions that guide interpretation of the scatterplots.
The exponential regression yields a negative base (b = â1.2607), which is not valid for realâvalued growth models. Reâexamine the fitting procedure.
Rather than manual substitution that led to misâfitting curves, rely on GDC regression outputs early while still showing algebraic understanding.
Section numbering repeats â2.â for two distinct headings, which could confuse readers. Ensure each level has a unique, sequential number in the table of contents.
The introduction effectively uses WHO and national statistics to contextualize smoking as a public health issue, demonstrating personal engagement with realâworld data.
The assumption of data accuracy is stated without critical evaluation of source reliability or potential biases. Discuss possible limitations in data collection to deepen reflection.
In Table 1 the function notation â(f)â appears without definition. Explicitly define f and all variables so readers can follow mathematical conventions.
The notation in ây = ax2 + bx + câ and ây = a Ã bxâ is incorrect. Use superscripts for powers (e.g. $ax^2$) and verify algebraic forms.
The evaluation in section 3 mentions RÂ² values but does not show their derivation. Including calculation or GDC output would deepen critical analysis.
The overlay of four regression types is an excellent use of multiple representations to compare model fits.
Axes in Figure 3 lack explicit labels and units. Always label axes to ensure clarity of quantitative information.
The percentage error formula is incorrect: it multiplies by the exact value rather than 100. The correct form is $\frac{|\text{approx}-\text{exact}|}{\text{exact}}\times100\,\%$.
The paired barâchart captions for two figures in one block become confusing. Separate captions to enhance presentation and reader understanding.
Choosing the exponential regression based on percentage error staying below 10% shows sound quantitative reasoning for model selection.
The selection of a 10% error threshold is arbitrary without justification. Explain why this level was chosen to validate model accuracy.
The notation â0.98042866^{Â°}â mistakenly uses a degree symbol instead of the variable exponent x. Correct to $0.98042866^x$.
Reapplying the percentage error analysis to the deaths model reflects thoughtful methodology and consistency in evaluating model accuracy.
Figures 8â10 combine multiple captions in one block, which hinders clarity. Assign distinct figure numbers and captions sequentially.
Table 2 compares pack sizes of 10 (Mexico) to 20 (UK) without unit adjustment; these should be standardized or explained to ensure a valid comparison.
Figure numbering jumps to âFig. 13â without intervening numbers. Renumber figures sequentially to maintain logical flow.
Using a single fixed exchange rate ignores fluctuations over 2008â2020. Discuss how varying rates could affect model accuracy.
The adjusted coefficient exponent ââ49.7â appears without rationale for rounding or precision. Clarify the choice of decimal exponent form.
The calculation of the vertical stretch factor k is shown, but more explanation of its interpretation and rounding would strengthen the mathematical communication.
Linking the observed differences in Mexicoâs data to wider socioeconomic and healthcare contexts demonstrates substantial personal engagement.
The final adjusted regression curve for Mexicoâs deaths model is not explicitly shown. Include the resulting plot to confirm visual fit.
Noting that a multivariable regression could improve model accuracy shows thoughtful reflection on methodological limitations.
The conclusion section is clearly labeled and summarizes findings effectively, providing logical closure.
The stated limitations could be strengthened by estimating the potential impact of differing data sources and unmodeled variables.
